texto: So was it cultural imperialism for Westerners to criticize foot-binding and female infanticide? Perhaps. But it was also the right thing to do. If we believe firmly in certain values, such as the equality of all human beings regardless of color or gender, then we should not be afraid to stand up for them; it would be feckless to defer slavery, torture, foot-binding, honor killings, or genital cutting just because we believe in respecting other faiths or cultures.
autor: Nicholas D. Kristof and Sheryl WuDunn
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Nicholas D. Kristof and Sheryl WuDunn que provoca temas ligados a fe. Ao questionar “So was it cultural imperialism for Westerners to criticize foot-binding and female infanticide? P…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre fe, feminism, half-the-sky, com foco em termino e half-the-sky.

texto: [S]ex trafficking and mass rape should no more be seen as women's issues than slavery was a black issue or the Holocaust was a Jewish issue. These are all humanitarian concerns, transcending any one race, gender, or creed.
autor: Nicholas D. Kristof and Sheryl WuDunn
explicacao: Orientação direta de Nicholas D. Kristof and Sheryl WuDunn sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “[S]ex trafficking and mass rape should no more be seen as women's issues than slavery was a black…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, equality, feminism, útil em momentos de termino e feminism.
